turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure follow-up. I’ve also been getting some headaches lately.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good morning. Thanks for coming in. Let’s talk through both. How have you been feeling overall, and when did you last check your blood pressure?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Overall I’m okay. The blood pressure was checked about a month ago at a pharmacy, and it was still a bit high. The headaches started about two weeks ago.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: I see. Can you tell me what the headaches feel like, where they are, and how long they last?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: It’s more like a dull ache, mostly at the back of my head or across my forehead. It comes and goes. Usually not the whole day.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do you get any warning signs with them, like blurred vision, nausea, weakness, chest pain, or shortness of breath?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, nothing like that. No weakness or chest pain. Sometimes I feel a bit tense at work, but that’s it.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s helpful. Have you missed any doses of your amlodipine, or started any new medicines, supplements, or pain tablets?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I’ve missed one or two tablets, but not often. I only take paracetamol when the headache is annoying. No other medicines.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. Any side effects from the amlodipine, like ankle swelling, flushing, or dizziness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No swelling or dizziness. I’m tolerating it fine.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: And how have things been with sleep, stress, caffeine, alcohol, and exercise?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Sleep is not great during the week. Work is busy. I do drink coffee in the mornings, maybe two cups. Alcohol is only now and then. I’m mostly sitting at work, so not much exercise.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thanks. Given your blood pressure history, I’d also like to ask about home readings, if you have any, and whether there’s any family history of high blood pressure or diabetes.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: My mother had hypertension, and my father has type 2 diabetes. I don’t have a home machine, so I haven’t been checking at home.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Understood. I’m going to check your blood pressure here today and review your weight and general exam. If the reading is still above target, we may need to adjust treatment or arrange home or pharmacy monitoring. We’ll also talk about food, salt, and activity, because those can make a real difference.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Okay, that sounds fine. I’ve been trying to cut down on salt, but I know I could do more.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s a good start. Based on what you’ve told me, this sounds most likely like mildly uncontrolled hypertension with tension-type headaches, but I’d want to confirm with today’s readings and exam. For now, keep taking the amlodipine daily, use paracetamol sparingly, and avoid taking extra pain tablets too often. We’ll check your blood pressure now, and if needed I may increase the dose or add another medicine. Please come back sooner if you develop severe headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, weakness, vision changes, or if the headaches become sudden or much worse. Otherwise, let’s review the readings and plan follow-up in the next few weeks.
